questions.q: What is the primary purpose of a frequency distribution?
opts: (A) To show the relationship between variables
(B) To display the distribution of data in a graphical form
(C) To describe the central tendency of a data set
(D) To analyze the correlation between variables
questions.explanation: A frequency distribution is a table or graph that shows the distribution of data in a graphical form, making it easier to understand and visualize the data.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a discrete variable?
opts: (A) The number of students in a class
(B) The number of students in a class
(C) The score obtained in a test
(D) The weight of a student
questions.explanation: A discrete variable is a variable that can take on specific, distinct values. In this case, the number of students in a class is a discrete variable.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating the mean of a data set?
opts: (A) Mean = (Sum of all values + 1) / (Number of values)
(B) Mean = (Sum of all values) / (Number of values)
(C) Mean = (Sum of all values - 1) / (Number of values)
(D) Mean = (Sum of all values + 2) / (Number of values)
questions.explanation: The formula for calculating the mean of a data set is the sum of all values divided by the number of values.

questions.q: What is the difference between a population and a sample?
opts: (A) A population is a small group of people, while a sample is a large group of people
(B) A population is a large group of people, while a sample is a small group of people
(C) A population is a group of people with similar characteristics, while a sample is a group of people with different characteristics
(D) A population is a group of people with different characteristics, while a sample is a group of people with similar characteristics
questions.explanation: A population is a large group of people, while a sample is a smaller group of people selected from the population.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a histogram?
opts: (A) To show the relationship between variables
(B) To display the distribution of data in a graphical form
(C) To describe the central tendency of a data set
(D) To analyze the correlation between variables
questions.explanation: A histogram is a graphical representation of the distribution of data, used to display the frequency of different values in a data set.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a skewed distribution?
opts: (A) A normal distribution
(B) A uniform distribution
(C) A bell-shaped distribution
(D) A distribution with most of the values concentrated on one side of the mean
questions.explanation: A skewed distribution is a distribution where most of the values are concentrated on one side of the mean, rather than being evenly distributed around the mean.

questions.q: What is the mode of a data set?
opts: (A) The value that appears most frequently in the data set
(B) The value that appears least frequently in the data set
(C) The value that is the mean of the data set
(D) The value that is the median of the data set
questions.explanation: The mode of a data set is the value that appears most frequently in the data set.

questions.q: What is the difference between a bar chart and a histogram?
opts: (A) A bar chart shows the frequency of different values, while a histogram shows the distribution of data
(B) A bar chart shows the distribution of data, while a histogram shows the frequency of different values
(C) A bar chart shows the relationship between variables, while a histogram shows the central tendency of a data set
(D) A bar chart shows the correlation between variables, while a histogram shows the distribution of data
questions.explanation: A bar chart shows the frequency of different values, while a histogram shows the distribution of data.

questions.q: What is the median of a data set?
opts: (A) The middle value of the data set when it is arranged in order
(B) The value that is the mean of the data set
(C) The value that appears most frequently in the data set
(D) The value that appears least frequently in the data set
questions.explanation: The median of a data set is the middle value of the data set when it is arranged in order.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a scatter plot?
opts: (A) To show the relationship between variables
(B) To display the distribution of data in a graphical form
(C) To describe the central tendency of a data set
(D) To analyze the correlation between variables
questions.explanation: A scatter plot is a graphical representation of the relationship between two variables.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a continuous variable?
opts: (A) The number of students in a class
(B) The score obtained in a test
(C) The weight of a student
(D) The height of a student
questions.explanation: A continuous variable is a variable that can take on any value within a given range. In this case, the weight of a student is a continuous variable.

questions.q: What is the range of a data set?
opts: (A) The difference between the largest and smallest values in the data set
(B) The average of the largest and smallest values in the data set
(C) The sum of the largest and smallest values in the data set
(D) The product of the largest and smallest values in the data set
questions.explanation: The range of a data set is the difference between the largest and smallest values in the data set.

questions.q: Which of the following is a measure of skewness?
opts: (A) Mean
(B) Median
(C) Mode
(D) Interquartile range
questions.explanation: The mean is a measure of skewness, as it can be affected by extreme values in the data set.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating the variance of a data set?
opts: (A) Variance = (Sum of squared deviations from the mean) / (Number of values)
(B) Variance = (Sum of squared deviations from the median) / (Number of values)
(C) Variance = (Sum of squared deviations from the mode) / (Number of values)
(D) Variance = (Sum of squared deviations from the range) / (Number of values)
questions.explanation: The formula for calculating the variance of a data set is the sum of squared deviations from the mean divided by the number of values.

questions.q: What is the difference between a parametric and non-parametric test?
opts: (A) A parametric test assumes a normal distribution of the data, while a non-parametric test does not assume a normal distribution
(B) A parametric test does not assume a normal distribution of the data, while a non-parametric test assumes a normal distribution
(C) A parametric test is used for small sample sizes, while a non-parametric test is used for large sample sizes
(D) A parametric test is used for continuous data, while a non-parametric test is used for categorical data
questions.explanation: A parametric test assumes a normal distribution of the data, while a non-parametric test does not assume a normal distribution.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a non-parametric test?
opts: (A) t-test
(B) ANOVA
(C) Chi-square test
(D) Sign test
questions.explanation: The sign test is a non-parametric test used to compare the median of two groups.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a confidence interval?
opts: (A) To estimate the population mean
(B) To estimate the population proportion
(C) To compare the means of two groups
(D) To test a hypothesis
questions.explanation: A confidence interval is used to estimate the population mean based on a sample of data.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of sampling bias?
opts: (A) Selection bias
(B) Information bias
(C) Confounding variable bias
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Selection bias, information bias, and confounding variable bias are all types of sampling bias.

questions.q: What is the concept of statistical significance?
opts: (A) The probability that the observed results are due to chance
(B) The probability that the observed results are due to a real effect
(C) The probability that the observed results are due to a sampling error
(D) The probability that the observed results are due to a measurement error
questions.explanation: Statistical significance is the probability that the observed results are due to chance, rather than a real effect.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a Bayesian inference?
opts: (A) Frequentist inference
(B) Bayesian inference
(C) Classical inference
(D) Empirical Bayes inference
questions.explanation: Bayesian inference is a statistical approach that uses prior knowledge and updating with new data to make inferences.

questions.q: What is the concept of p-value?
opts: (A) The probability that the observed results are due to chance
(B) The probability that the observed results are due to a real effect
(C) The probability that the observed results are due to a sampling error
(D) The probability that the observed results are due to a measurement error
questions.explanation: The p-value is the probability that the observed results are due to chance, and is used to determine statistical significance.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a robust statistical method?
opts: (A) Least squares regression
(B) Maximum likelihood estimation
(C) Bootstrap sampling
(D) Jackknife resampling
questions.explanation: Bootstrap sampling is a robust statistical method that uses repeated sampling with replacement to estimate the variability of a statistic.
